mapping.a_element: Algebraic Birkhoff Decomposition in the Connes-Kreimer Hopf algebra
mapping.b_element: BPHZ renormalization procedure in the Connes-Kreimer Hopf algebra of Feynman graphs

mapping.a_element: differential algebra variation of Birkhoff decomposition

mapping.a_element: renormalization process of multiple zeta values
mapping.b_element: renormalization in the context of algebro-geometric Feynman rules
Connes-Kreimer Hopf algebra of Feynman graphs

mapping.a_element: decomposition applied to study multiple zeta values
mapping.b_element: Feynman rules factoring through Grothendieck rings or polynomial rings

brief_justification: Both papers operate within the Connes-Kreimer Hopf algebra framework for renormalization, where the core machinery involves decompositions (Birkhoff or algebro-geometric) of characters in the Hopf algebra. The differential Birkhoff decomposition in A corresponds to the construction of algebro-geometric Feynman rules in B, both serving as methods to regularize or renormalize divergent quantities (multiple zeta values or Feynman integrals). The shared underlying mechanism is the use of algebraic structures (Hopf algebras) to systematically handle renormalization.
